conflictOfInterest.details: Founding-stage isolation check: the accountable principal VSBDev operates the authoring, review, and finalization agents, so principal-level independence is absent. Agent-level role isolation was strictly maintained: no authoring history, other review output, or adjudication draft was read, and no other EmbeddedKnowledge role script was run during this review execution.

findings.finding: First-read clarity: The orientation scene introduces a concrete, relatable fictional student (Noor) experiencing sleep and concentration issues before introducing any learning objectives or technical vocabulary. A novice learner at the declared level can easily understand what is being studied and why it matters after one read, successfully passing the first-read gate.

Worked example quality: The boundary worked example (worked-example-noor-boundary) is exceptionally thorough, structured around the four-question scope test and covering all nine steps required by the Content Standard (including plan, execution, independent check, and self-explanation prompt).

Scaffolded practice: The practice scene displays an effective fading support sequence, moving from a structured fill-in-the-blank completion problem to a scenario-based boundary problem (Mina) and culminating in a learner-generated independent problem.

Assessment transfer: The final assessment item (item-transfer-policy-map) is a strong, novel policy-level transfer problem that avoids repeating worked situations. The criteria and scoring rubric are fully aligned with the target objectives.

Title vocabulary mismatch: The lesson title is declared as 'Welcome to Psychiatry: Mind, Brain, Person, Society', which uses the term 'Society' for the highest level. However, the core conceptual map (Scene 2) uses 'System' (alongside 'Relationship' and 'Culture') to capture the social context. While aligned with the syllabus name, using 'Society' in the title versus 'System' in the map may cause slight cognitive friction for a novice learner.
findings.resolution: No immediate changes required as it is aligned with course syllabus, but a minor note in Scene 1 or 2 connecting 'Society' to the systemic, relational, and cultural lenses would improve first-read coherence.

limitations: Expert-simulation review: This review represents a single-pass advisory input by a simulated learning-design expert under standard-lesson-v3 guidelines. No real learner testing was conducted.
Conflict of interest: The same GitHub principal (VSBDev) is responsible for the authoring and review run operators. Agent-level isolation was maintained, but principal-level independence is absent.

Scope restriction: The review is strictly bounded to learning-design metrics (e.g., clarity, coherence, practice, alignment, and transfer). Scientific accuracy, rights audits, and final typesetting checks are excluded.

Technical verification limits: No rendered build environment, screen reader, or keyboard navigation pathways were evaluated physically; findings are based solely on the static Markdown and JSON source files.
